text.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the github-actions-cicd methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Pin every third-party action to a full 40-character commit SHA — never @v4, @main or any mutable tag.
rule.rationale: Mutable tags are the documented supply-chain attack surface; only a SHA is immutable.

rule.statement: Set permissions at workflow or job level with only the scopes actually needed; default contents: read.
rule.rationale: GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to write — every job that does not need write is over-privileged.

rule.statement: Use OIDC (id-token: write + cloud federation) to mint short-lived cloud credentials instead of storing long-lived secrets as repo secrets.
rule.rationale: Long-lived cloud secrets in repo storage are the dominant CI/CD breach root cause.

rule.statement: Pass GitHub event context (issue title, PR body) to shell via environment variables, not inline expression interpolation.
rule.rationale: Inline ${{ }} expansion in run scripts is the canonical RCE vector when the event content is attacker-controlled.

rule.statement: Never run pull_request_target with checkout of fork code without explicit trust verification — gate on label / member / signed commit.
rule.rationale: pull_request_target runs with secrets; checking out untrusted fork code there grants the attacker secrets.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
